page: Amazon Web Services c6gn.16xlarge is an arm64-based compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor architecture running at 2.5 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but it features a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities and high-throughput networking performance. The server achieves a strong rating in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and software compilation via Clang. It also demonstrates strong performance in cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput. Conversely, single-core performance is average, and single-threaded tasks such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression register as weak. Large-block uncached memory read and write operations, along with database operations and medium-sized LLM inference, fall into the average performance tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARMv8 architecture provides a high physical core density and high network bandwidth relative to its resource profile, though the lack of local storage requires external block storage solutions. The instance is suited for multi-threaded compute workloads, high-throughput network applications, software compilation, and distributed caching systems like Redis. It is less suited for single-threaded processing or large-scale LLM inference due to weaker single-core and specialized instruction benchmarks.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.16xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized server instance powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 128.0 GB of RAM, 100 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, high cached memory bandwidth, and high throughput for Redis SET operations and static web serving. However, single-core performance and single-threaded compression tasks are relatively weak. Qualitatively, the instance offers high physical core density and network capacity for its resource class. It is suited for multi-threaded compute tasks, network-intensive applications, software compilation, and caching workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.16xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized server with 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for multi-threaded and network-heavy workloads.

tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized server featuring sixty-four dedicated physical cores and high-speed one-hundred gigabit baseline network bandwidth.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores at 2.5 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory with 2.0 GB per core allocation
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth utilizing the Nitro hypervisor
Strong multi-core CPU, software compilation, and Redis performance
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Suited for multi-threaded compute, caching, and network-intensive workloads
